---
title: History of collection activities within the region
weight: 2
knit: (function(input, ...) { source(here::here("R/knit_render.R"), local = TRUE)$value(input, ...) })
gridVisible: true
---

## History of collection activities within the region

While Indigenous knowledge of Átl’ḵa7tsem's plant life traces back to time immemorial, Western scientific
documentation of the region's flora dates to 1897, with the early collection efforts of James Anderson and
Albert J. Hill. Over the last century and a half, early efforts to document the regional flora were most
concentrated in the 1920s, and from the 1960s through the 1980s, before expanding significantly in the
21st century with the advent of iNaturalist, with many thousands of observations made by community
members through the <a href = "https://www.squamishenvironment.ca/projects/biodiversity-squamish/">Howe Sound Biodiversity project</a>.

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# According to https://stackoverflow.com/questions/5031630/how-to-source-r-file-saved-using-utf-8-encoding this strange strategy is now necessary since the 
# file contains a character outside the Unicode BMP
eval(parse("scripts/Historical_analysis_mapbox.R", encoding="UTF-8"))
heatMap
speciesPlot
```
